hero.title: Backend Developer — .NET · TypeScript · Java
hero.subtitle: Building secure, observable, and maintainable APIs for real-world systems.

about.heading: About
about.bio1: I'm a backend developer focused on building APIs that are secure by design, easy to observe in production, and maintainable over time. I work primarily with .NET/C#, TypeScript/NestJS, and Java/Spring Boot.
about.bio2: My approach is spec-first: I think through failure cases and invariants before writing a line of code. I care about structured logging, correlation IDs, and making systems that behave predictably under pressure.

security.title: Security-First
security.description: Auth, input validation, and access control are built into every layer — not added as afterthoughts.

observability.title: Observability by Design
observability.description: Structured logging, correlation IDs, and meaningful error responses make production debugging tractable.

spec.title: Spec Before Code
spec.description: Failure cases and invariants are defined before implementation. Tests drive the design.

projects_page.heading: Projects
projects_page.subtitle: Same domain. Different stacks. Each one built to demonstrate architecture depth.

helpdesk-dotnet.shortDescription: Helpdesk ticket system built with Modular Monolith, Clean Architecture, EF Core, and PostgreSQL.
helpdesk-dotnet.description: Helpdesk domain built in C# .NET 10 as a Modular Monolith. Implements a ticket state machine (Open → In Progress → Resolved / Cancelled) with role-based access control, SLA deadline tracking, domain events, and structured logging. Refresh token rotation with Argon2id and family invalidation. Auth audit events. 193 tests: 59 unit, 128 integration (TestContainers + real PostgreSQL), 6 architecture. API documentation via Scalar.
helpdesk-dotnet.architectureSummary: Modular Monolith with Clean Architecture per module. Four modules: Tickets (core domain), Identity (auth + sessions), SLA (deadline engine + scoring), Notifications (contracts). Domain layer has zero infrastructure dependencies. State transitions enforced at domain layer only. Use Cases injected directly via DI — no Mediator, no command bus.

helpdesk-nestjs.shortDescription: Same helpdesk domain reimplemented in TypeScript using NestJS, Fastify, Prisma, and EventEmitter2.
helpdesk-nestjs.description: TypeScript implementation of the helpdesk domain on NestJS + Fastify. Uses Prisma as ORM, EventEmitter2 for domain events (dispatched after persistence), and Vitest + TestContainers for integration tests against a real PostgreSQL instance. 248 tests covering the full feature set: tickets, SLA, audit events, attachments, and auth with JWT rotation and Argon2id. Feature-complete with the .NET version.
helpdesk-nestjs.architectureSummary: Modular NestJS structure with dedicated modules per domain area. Use Cases injected directly via DI — no Mediator, no command bus. Domain logic isolated from the framework. EventEmitter2 dispatches events synchronously after persistence.

helpdesk-java.shortDescription: Java implementation with Spring Boot, JPA, and JUnit 5 test coverage.
helpdesk-java.description: Java implementation of the helpdesk domain using Spring Boot 3 and Spring Data JPA. Applies the same architectural decisions — Clean Architecture, spec-first domain invariants, role-based authorization — in a strongly-typed enterprise stack.
helpdesk-java.architectureSummary: Layered architecture following Spring Boot conventions. Repository pattern via Spring Data JPA. Domain invariants enforced in the service layer. JUnit 5 for all business rule coverage.
